title: Perdure Agent Context (generic)
description: The full, vendor-neutral operating context an external coding agent reads before each edit (`perdure guard context --for-agent generic --json`). It is the structured front door to one guard session: what you may touch (`allowed_files`), what you must not (`forbidden_files` / `scope_violations`), what Perdure will run (`allowed_commands` / `required_commands`), what has changed (`changed_files`), the latest command evidence (`latest_receipts`), the current failure, the `done_condition`, and the single `next_action` with its exact `recommended_command`. Pin the contract with `schema`.

schema.description: The versioned contract tag, e.g. perdure.agent-context.v1.

agent.description: Which agent shape this is rendered for (currently always generic).

goal.description: The goal name resolved from the repo's Perdurefile.

status.description: An agent-facing status label: editing | verified | finalized | aborted | blocked.

allowed_files.description: The fs.write globs the agent may edit.

forbidden_files.description: Files you have changed that are outside allowed_files — revert these. A flat companion to scope_violations, which carries the reasons.

allowed_commands.description: The only commands Perdure will run, matched verbatim.

required_commands.description: The commands that must pass for the goal to be verified.

changed_files.description: The change set since the session baseline, classified against the write scope.

out_of_scope.description: Changed files outside allowed_files (these block verify).

scope_violations.description: The live out-of-scope writes, with the reason and the scope that would have allowed each.

latest_receipts.description: The most recent receipt for each required command: did it pass, and where are its captured stdout/stderr artifacts.

current_failure.description: A summary of the most recent failing verify, or null.

done_condition.description: The exact check the agent must use to decide it is finished.

next_action.description: edit_then_verify | fix_scope_violation | run_verify | finalize | done | aborted | blocked.

recommended_command.description: The exact Perdure command to run next (empty when the run is terminal).

instructions.description: Short, ordered guidance lines for the current state.

verified.description: True only when every required command passed AND no out-of-scope file changed.
